headline: Survey at 0.7% completion with strong early sentiment but critical acceleration needed.
collection_status: 9 responses collected against 1,350 target represents severely lagging pace. Only 2 of 10 planned regions active (MARA, SONGWE). At current trajectory, survey will not reach minimum viable sample without immediate intervention.
key_findings: Volunteer respondents dominate (67% of sample) with minimal RC/ARC participation, skewing stakeholder representation toward field staff rather than management/coordination.
Partner satisfaction high (4.8/5) but scale readiness critically low (2.9/5), indicating enthusiasm without operational capacity to expand programs.

Top priority is new programs (56% of mentions) over strengthening existing KiuFunza model, suggesting either dissatisfaction with current approach or aspirational thinking disconnected from readiness.
Feedback emphasizes government integration, teacher incentives, and digital monitoring—operational pain points suggesting implementation gaps in current model.
flags: 8 regions completely inactive with zero responses; risk of geographic blind spots in final dataset and skewed regional insights.
Scale readiness at 2.9/5 contradicts expansion ambitions; urgent need to diagnose capacity constraints before pursuing new programs priority.
Respondent type imbalance suggests RC/ARC underengagement; without management perspectives, survey misses critical bottleneck analysis.
recommendation: Immediately launch targeted outreach to 8 inactive regions and prioritize RC/ARC recruitment to hit 15% sample completion within 2 weeks. Concurrently conduct rapid diagnostic on the scale readiness gap (2.9/5) to understand whether capacity constraints are resource, systems, or capability-driven before endorsing new program expansion.
